---
title: "ProSIFT -- Handoff File"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

**Last Updated:** 2026-04-07 (Module 05 edge case tested; documentation restructuring complete for all modules)

**Purpose:** Operational coordination across Claude sessions. Tracks current development state, active tasks, blockers, and data contracts. Updated every session.

**Key principle:** The Project Documentation (proSIFT_master_doc.Rmd) is the architectural reference. This handoff is purely operational -- it tracks *what is happening now*, not *why things are designed the way they are*. Reference the Project Documentation for explanations rather than restating them.

**Companion files (read on demand, not every session):**

- `proSIFT_session_log.Rmd` -- Append-only session history. Read when catching up on recent implementation work.
- `proSIFT_environment.Rmd` -- Platform, rsync, conda, git reference. Read when working with the cluster or committing.
- `proSIFT_file_map.Rmd` -- Directory tree and scripts reference. Read when creating or locating files.

---

### DOCUMENT MAINTENANCE RULES:
- **ADD** new information to appropriate sections; do not remove existing content unless archiving
- **EDIT** only when specific information needs correction
- **REFERENCE** the Project Documentation for design rationale (format: [See Project Documentation Section X.X: Title])

---

# 1. Claude Code Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read this Handoff File (all sections)
- Read the Project Documentation if instructed
- Check the Session Log (`proSIFT_session_log.Rmd`) to understand recent work

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- Follow the QC check protocol below -- pause between major steps, report metrics, and ask before proceeding
- When referencing architectural decisions or design rationale, ask the user to provide it if deeper context is needed
- When implementing cross-module interfaces, check "Integration Points" (Section 5) for data contracts
- If a module's expected inputs or outputs change during implementation, update Section 5 immediately
- Log work as you go in the "Current Session" section (Section 6) below

**AT SESSION END:**

Flush the current session to the session log, then clean up:

1. **Write to session log first:** Read only the first ~35 lines of `proSIFT_session_log.Rmd` to confirm the structure. Insert the contents of Section 6 (everything between `[SESSION START]` and `[SESSION END]`) immediately after the `# Active Session Log` header, above any existing entries. Do not read the rest of the file.
2. **Clear Section 6:** Replace the session content with the empty template (keep the `[SESSION START]` and `[SESSION END]` markers, remove everything between them).
3. **Update other sections as needed:**
   - Update "Current Development State" (Section 2) if module statuses changed
   - Move completed tasks to "Completed Tasks" (Section 3.3) with date
   - Update the File System Map (`proSIFT_file_map.Rmd`) if files were created, moved, or deleted
   - Flag any issues or questions in Section 4
   - If a significant design decision was made, note it and flag it for promotion to the Project History Decision Register (`proSIFT_project_history.Rmd`)

**Important:** Always write to the session log before clearing Section 6. If the session is interrupted, a duplicate entry is recoverable; a lost entry is not.

### QC Check Protocol (Mandatory)

- Pause between major implementation steps to verify outputs before proceeding
- Report QC metrics after each step -- do not continue silently
- Ask the user if results look reasonable before moving to the next part
- If any test fails or produces unexpected results, STOP and report

**Check-in format after major steps:**
```
Step [X] complete.
  Results:
  - Output file: [path]
  - [Key metric 1]: [value] (expected: [range])
  - [Key metric 2]: [value] (expected: [range])
  QC Status: [PASS / WARNING / FAIL]
  [Notes or concerns]
  Ready to proceed to Step [Y]. Continue? (y/n)
```

**Red flags to watch for and report:**

- Tests that pass when they should fail (or vice versa)
- Unexpected file sizes, row counts, or data dimensions
- Schema mismatches between modules (check Section 5)
- Warnings from statistical or data processing functions
- Discrepancies between what was specified and what the data supports
- Silent data loss (rows/columns dropped without explanation)

---

# 2. Current Development State

## 2.1 Phase Overview

| Phase | Description | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Core pipeline skeleton + pre-normalization QC | Complete | All Module 01 processes + Module 02 implemented and tested |
| Phase 2 | Normalization, imputation, statistical analysis, enrichment + QC report assembly | In progress | Modules 03, 04, and 05 complete. Module 06 (database queries) next. |
| Phase 3 | Database integration layer | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.3] |
| Phase 4 | Interactive frontend | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.4] |

**Current phase:** Phase 2 in progress.

**Current focus:** Module 03b (QC Report Assembly) implemented and tested (2026-04-07). Module 06 (Database Queries) design and implementation next.

## 2.2 Module Status

Tracks implementation status of individual modules within and across phases. A module may be: Not started, In progress, Functional (works but incomplete), Complete, or Blocked.

| Module | Phase | Status | Last Touched | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Project structure / repo setup | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | main.nf, nextflow.config, conf/base.config, workflows/, modules/local/, bin/ |
| nextflow.config + params.yml schema | 1 | Functional | 2026-03-26 | local/pbs/conda/singularity profiles; cluster path needs updating before first cluster run |
| Conda environment | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-25 | environment.yml finalized; prosift env exists locally; not yet created on cluster |
| Data preparation scripts | Pre-1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | 3 scripts in scripts/; samplesheet.csv generated; 12 per-run input directories |
| Module 01: Input Validation & ID Mapping | 1 | Complete | 2026-04-06 | All processes (4.1-4.10) implemented and tested. 5 scripts in bin/. Process 4.7 (ortholog mapping) now populated via BioMart. missingness_report.py Plot 2 denominator annotation added. |
| Module 02: Pre-normalization QC/EDA | 1 | Complete | 2026-04-06 | bin/prenorm_qc.py; 18 outputs (added density plot); clustermap with dendrogram; CTXcyto benchmark verified |
| Module 03: Normalization & Imputation | 2 | Complete | 2026-04-06 | NORMALIZE + IMPUTE both implemented and tested. Full pipeline test all 12 runs, 0 NaN remaining. NORMALIZE now produces 4 diagnostic plot types (added density, clustermap replaces heatmap). |
| QC Report Assembly | 2 | Functional | 2026-04-07 | bin/qc_report_assembly.py + Nextflow module. CTXcyto benchmark tested. Prerequisite imputation mask implemented. |
| Module 04: Differential Abundance (DEqMS) | 2 | Complete | 2026-03-30 | bin/differential_abundance.py; limma+DEqMS via rpy2; 7 outputs; tested all 12 runs. |
| Module 05: ORA / GSEA enrichment | 2 | Complete | 2026-03-31 | bin/enrichment.py; gseapy ORA + preranked GSEA; GO_BP + Reactome; lollipop + running score plots; standalone tested. |
| Module 06: Database queries (all 5 databases) | 3 | Not started | -- | -- |
| Module 07: Results assembly (SQLite) | 3 | Not started | -- | -- |
| Module 08: Interactive frontend | 4 | Not started | -- | Tech selection deferred |
| Singularity definition | 1 | Not started | -- | Dependency list established; ready to write .def file |

## 2.3 Nextflow Process Status

Tracks which Nextflow processes exist and whether they are functional. Populated as processes are implemented.

| Process Name | Module | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| VALIDATE_INPUTS | 01 | Functional | bin/validate_inputs.py; tested all 12 runs |
| FILTER_PROTEINS | 01 | Functional | bin/filter_proteins.py; tested all 12 runs |
| UNIPROT_MAPPING | 01 | Functional | bin/uniprot_mapping.py; paginated JSON API; schema v2 cache; BioMart Layer 2 ortholog mapping; mirror fallback; CTXcyto standalone tested (2026-04-02) |
| MISSINGNESS_REPORT | 01 | Functional | bin/missingness_report.py; errorStrategy 'ignore'; tested all 12 runs |
| PRENORM_QC | 02 | Functional | bin/prenorm_qc.py; 17 outputs; tested all 12 runs |
| NORMALIZE | 03 | Functional | bin/normalize.py; 12 emit channels; tested all 12 runs (2026-03-30) |
| IMPUTE | 03 | Functional | bin/impute.py; 9 emit channels (added imputation_mask 2026-04-07); tested all 12 runs (2026-03-30) |
| DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE | 04 | Functional | bin/differential_abundance.py; limma + DEqMS via rpy2; volcano + MA plots; 7 named emit channels; tested all 12 runs (2026-03-30) |
| QC_REPORT_ASSEMBLY | 03b | Functional | bin/qc_report_assembly.py; 13 Plotly plots; single HTML output; errorStrategy 'ignore'; CTXcyto benchmark tested (2026-04-07) |
| ENRICHMENT | 05 | Functional | bin/enrichment.py; gseapy ORA + preranked GSEA; lollipop plots (Plotly PNG+HTML) + GSEA running score plots; 9 named emit channels; standalone tested (2026-03-31) |

---

# 3. Task Queue

## 3.1 Pending Tasks

> **Entry template:**
>
> ### [Task Title]
> - **Assigned to:** [Claude.ai / Claude Code]
> - **Priority:** [High / Medium / Low]
> - **Phase:** [1 / 2 / 3 / 4]
> - **Module:** [Which module this belongs to]
> - **Description:** [What needs to be done]
> - **Context/References:** [Relevant Project Documentation sections, prior session log entries]
> - **Acceptance criteria:** [How to know when it's done]
> - **Dependencies:** [Other tasks or modules that must be complete first]

### Replace Missingness Heatmap with SINGLE-GROUP Direction Summary
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** Low
- **Phase:** 2
- **Module:** Module 01 (Process 4.10 MISSINGNESS_REPORT and Process 4.4 validation report)
- **Description:** Replace Plot 3 (missingness heatmap for filtered-out proteins) in the missingness report with a SINGLE-GROUP direction summary. The heatmap's information (binary detected/missing across 6 samples for ~400-500 filtered proteins) is largely redundant with the category labels themselves and has low information density. The replacement surfaces more actionable information: how many SINGLE-GROUP proteins are detected in each group, revealing biological or technical asymmetry between conditions. Implementation: (1) Add two lines to the validation report text block under the Single-group count showing per-group breakdown (e.g., "Detected in WT only: 168 / Detected in KO only: 69"). (2) In the missingness report, replace the heatmap with either a stacked or split bar within the existing Plot 1 category bar chart, or drop Plot 3 entirely if the text summary is sufficient. (3) Update Module 01 spec Section 4.10 plot descriptions.
- **Context/References:** Module 01 spec Section 4.10 (MISSINGNESS_REPORT process, Plot 3 specification). Discussion in Claude.ai session 2026-03-31 concluded the heatmap is a visual restatement of information already conveyed by category labels, while direction counts answer a distinct question (condition asymmetry) in a more compact form.
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  1. Validation report text block includes per-group SINGLE-GROUP counts
  2. Missingness report Plot 3 (heatmap) removed or replaced
  3. Module 01 spec Section 4.10 updated to reflect the change
  4. Remaining plots (1, 2, 4) unaffected
- **Dependencies:** The PARTIAL category has been implemented (2026-04-06). This task should account for PARTIAL proteins in the direction summary as well.

## 3.2 Decisions and Designs for Review

> Implementation choices, design tradeoffs, or prototype outputs produced by Claude Code
> that need user or Claude.ai sign-off before building further.

None currently.

## 3.3 Completed Tasks

*Detailed completed task log (2026-03-20 through 2026-03-30) archived. Summary: data prep scripts, Module 01 (all processes), Module 02, Module 03 (NORMALIZE + IMPUTE), full pipeline test all 12 runs, Module 04 design. See Project History (`proSIFT_project_history.Rmd`) Section 2 (Development Log) for details.*

- Ortholog Mapping (Layer 2, Process 4.7) implemented and tested -- 2026-04-02 -- bin/uniprot_mapping.py extended with BioMart query, mirror fallback, version suffix stripping, cache schema versioning. CTXcyto: 97.0% one_to_one, 1.1% one_to_many, 1.9% no_ortholog. See session log 2026-04-02 for details.
- Module 01/02 QC Visualization Enhancements -- 2026-04-06 -- (A) Per-sample KDE density plots added to prenorm_qc.py. (B) Correlation heatmap converted to clustermap with dendrogram (1-r distance, average linkage). (C) Denominator annotations: missingness report Plot 2 subtitle shows total protein count; prenorm_qc.py HTML report notes post-filter denominator. (D) Module 02 spec updated (Section 4.2b, 4.5, 5); Module 01 spec Section 4.10 updated. CTXcyto benchmark tested, all outputs verified.
- Post-normalization density plot and clustermap added to NORMALIZE -- 2026-04-06 -- `plot_density()` moved to shared `prosift_plot_utils.py`. `normalize.py` now produces `postnorm_density` and `postnorm_clustermap` (replacing `postnorm_correlation_heatmap`). Nextflow NORMALIZE and PRENORM_QC modules updated (new density channels, clustermap rename). Module 03a spec Section 4.5 revised (4 plot types). CTXcyto benchmark tested.
- Imputation mask added to IMPUTE process -- 2026-04-07 -- `bin/impute.py` extended with `build_imputation_mask()` and `write_imputation_mask()`. New output `{run_id}.imputation_mask.parquet` (per-cell: observed/mnar/mar). IMPUTE Nextflow module updated (new emit channel). Module 03a spec Section 2.2 updated. CTXcyto benchmark: mask shape (8403, 7), categories match imputation_summary exactly.
- Module 03b QC Report Assembly implemented -- 2026-04-07 -- New `bin/qc_report_assembly.py` (15 CLI args, 13 Plotly plots, 6 report sections). New `modules/local/qc_report_assembly/main.nf` (errorStrategy 'ignore'). Re-renders all plots from upstream data; CSS grid paired layouts; CV density plot (new). CTXcyto benchmark tested: 2.6 MB report, all numbers verified against upstream files.
- PARTIAL detection filter category implemented -- 2026-04-06 -- Option B (fifth category) chosen. Code: `filter_proteins.py` (classify + report), `missingness_report.py` (order, colors, heatmap), `impute.py` (mixed MNAR/MAR routing). Specs: Module 01 Section 4.4 + Decision Register, Module 03a Section 4.7. Category order: PASSED-PARTIAL-SINGLE-GROUP-SPARSE-ABSENT. CTXcyto rerun: 263 PARTIAL proteins (2.9%), previously misclassified. Module 01 fully rerun for CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO (all 4 processes). Stale results_fulltest/ and results_test/ directories deleted.

---

# 4. Open Questions and Blockers

## 4.1 Blockers

> Issues that are preventing progress on a specific module or task.
>
> **Entry format:** [DATE opened] [Module affected] [Description] [Status/progress]

None currently.

## 4.2 Open Questions

> Questions that need resolution but are not blocking immediate work.
>
> **Entry format:** [DATE opened] [Question] [Status/progress]

- 2026-03-26 -- UniProt cache dir on cluster must be on persistent storage. Currently `{params.outdir}/cache/id_mapping`. Confirm `params.outdir` resolves to a persistent mount (home2 or scratch, not a temp FS) before first cluster run. If outdir is on scratch, the cache may be deleted between submissions, defeating the point.
- 2026-03-26 -- Input usability: current workflow requires 3 preprocessing scripts + samplesheet + per-run params/metadata before pipeline runs. Acceptable for development; not suitable for bench scientists. The usability layer sits entirely outside the pipeline and can be rewritten at any point without architectural changes. Deferred to post-Phase 1. [See also Project Documentation Section 6.5]

## 4.3 Resolved Questions

*Archived. Prior resolved questions (benchmark dataset selection, tools survey, conda path workaround, samplesheet generation, VSN package availability, VSN+NA handling, MinProb reproducibility) all captured in Project Documentation or module specs.*

---

# 5. Current Session

This section holds the in-progress session log entry. It is written during the session and flushed to `proSIFT_session_log.Rmd` at session end (see Section 1, "AT SESSION END" for the flush procedure).

[No active session]
